steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 1 calls root-to-root separation "an essential step" and Text 2 says Chen and colleagues took "the necessary precaution of separating the plants' root systems". Despite disagreeing on whether Chen's full design succeeds, both authors treat separating root systems as a required foundation for testing CMN nutrient transfer.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Zhao and colleagues' study did not find convincing evidence of nutrient transfer between individual plants.: invents a point neither author raises. ✗
  • Choice B — Excluding root-to-root transfer of nutrients between plants is sufficient to evaluate whether any observed nutrient transfer involved a CMN.: both authors say the step is necessary, not that it alone guarantees a valid result. ✗
  • Choice C — It is impossible to determine whether a CMN is the mechanism for any observed nutrient transfer unless root-to-root transfer is precluded.: states exactly the prerequisite both texts share. ✓
  • Choice D — A barrier that is impervious to both roots and fungal strands is necessary to examine nutrient transfer via a CMN.: adds a condition neither author mentions. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
